---
title: "5f: Affinity Inference"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Summary

Affinity inference uses Non-Negative Least Squares (NNLS) to estimate phylon
affinities for new query genomes based on the precomputed NMF basis matrix (L).
New strains are also classified into MASH clusters via 5-nearest-neighbor
majority vote.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os

df = pd.read_csv("../output/data/5f_affinity_summary.csv")
for _, row in df.iterrows():
    print(f"{row['metric']}: {row['value']}")
```

## MASH Cluster Assignments

```{python}
mc_path = "../output/data/5f_mash_clusters.csv"
if os.path.exists(mc_path):
    mc = pd.read_csv(mc_path, index_col=0)
    mc
else:
    print("MASH cluster assignments not available.")
```

## Affinity Heatmap

```{python}
#| output: asis
hm_path = "../output/figures/5f_affinity_heatmap.png"
if os.path.exists(hm_path):
    print(f"![Inferred affinities for new strains]({hm_path}){{width=80%}}")
else:
    print("Heatmap not available (workflow outputs missing).")
```
